mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over the past three months signals accelerating economic activity, which raises expectations for future short-term rates and inflation, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward yield change is not significantly positive when INDPRO is above its 6-month moving average (t-test p > 0.10), or the Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO monthly changes and subsequent yield changes is below 0.10 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6.0% from a level above 6% signals labor market tightening, which leads to wage pressures and expectations of Fed tightening, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after the trigger UNRATE < 6.0%, the 20-day yield change is negative more than 60% of subsequent instances, or the average yield change is not statistically different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero (accommodative) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates month-over-month, the combination signals that the Fed will eventually need to tighten monetary policy, which raises long-term yields as the market prices in future rate hikes over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 60-day yield change is negative in over half of the cases, or the predictive performance (information coefficient) is less than 0.05 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession expansion phase, rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand, which boosts growth expectations and leads to higher long-term bond yields as risk appetite improves.
regime_hint: post-recession expansion phase (GDPC1 growing, UNRATE above 5% but declining)
failure_condition: The positive relationship between RSAFS growth and 20-day yield changes fails to hold in out-of-sample periods where the economy is also in a post-recession expansion; Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) signals rising construction activity and long-term economic investment, which raises the natural rate of interest and pushes the 10-year yield higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST monthly changes and 60-day forward yield changes is less than 0.10 or statistically insignificant over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: If retail sales (RSAFS) exceed $450 billion (a high level relative to recent history), it signals unusually strong consumer spending that will lead to overheating fears and a rapid upward adjustment in bond yields over the following week.
failure_condition: Following the trigger, the average 5-day yield change is negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate (proportion of positive yield moves) is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) increase by more than 500,000 and retail sales (RSAFS) increase by more than 1% month-over-month, the combined signal of robust job growth and strong consumer spending reinforces expectations of a tightening cycle, lifting the 10-year yield over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 500 and RSAFS monthly change > 1%
regime_hint: recovery or expansion with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: The signal fails when the joint condition holds but yields decline in more than 50% of cases, or the average yield change is not greater than the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPIAUCSL index below 240), an increase in inflation expectations, proxied by a monthly rise in CPIAUCSL, signals that the Fed may need to remove accommodation sooner than expected, causing long-term yields to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive relationship between monthly CPI increases and 20-day yield changes is reversed or insignificant during periods when CPIAUCSL is below 240; Spearman correlation < 0.05.
